title: aCGH Hybridization & wash
steps: Turn on hybridization oven 65 °C Turn on heat block 95 °C Turn on heat block 37 °C
Mix Cy3 and Cy5 samples
Mix the following components in 1.5 centrifuge tubes * Cy3/Cy5 gDNA mixture 39 µl * Cot-1 DNA (1.3 µg/µl) 5 µl * Agilent 10x blocking agent 11 µl * Agilent 2x Hybridization buffer 55 µl Total110 µl
Mix sample by pipetting, by careful not to create any bubbles.
Spin down with maximum speed for a few seconds.
Heat for 00:03:00 at 95 °C and incubate immediately thereafter for 00:30:00 at 37 °C .
Leave samples in heat block, spin down for 00:01:00 at maximum speed.
Load gasket slide in hybridization chamber and dispense 100 µl hyb mix in a drag and drop manner (use a 200 µl pipette, be careful not to create any bubbles)
Place microarray slide – active side down – and assemble chamber.
Check for immobile air bubbles by rotating the assembled hybridization chamber. If necessary, slam side of the chamber on table to loosen the air bubbles.
Place assembled chamber in the hybridization oven ( 65 °C at 20 rpm) and make sure to balance with empty chambers.
Hybridize for 24:00:00 , 65 °C at 20 rpm.
Turn on Agilent Slide Scanner
Wash buffer dishes with distilled water and blow dry with pressurized air.

Add buffers to dishes and have tin foil covering ready.- Wash a maximum of 5 Agilent slides at the same time. DishMethod BufferTemp (ºC)Time (min) #1 Disassembly Buffer 1 RT - #2 1st wash stepBuffer 1 RT 5(stirrer) #3 2nd wash stepBuffer 2 37 1(stirrer & hot plate) #4 Dry step AcetonitrileRT 1
Separate gasket slide from microarray slide in dish #1 (buffer 1)
Place slides in holding rack and was in dish #2 for 00:05:00 , rotation of stirrer is switched ON!
Cover dish with tin foil.
Start the " Scan Control Software" For Agilent 180k, select the “AgilentG3_CGH” Option
Click on “Set Values” to confirm all settings
Move holding rack to dish #3 (buffer 2) and wash for 00:01:00 , rotation of stirrer and heating is switched ON!
Move holding rack to dish #4 (acetonitrile) leave for 00:01:00 .
Remove slide gently with tweezers, thus preventing the formation of droplets.
Place slide in holder and load into the carousel of the scanner Agilent Slide Scanner scans through the back of the slide (this side must contain the numeric barcode)
Open “feature extraction” and check TIFF file

Click Scan Slot m-n on the Scan Control main window The letter m represents the Start slot where your first slide is located, and the letter n represents the End slot where your last slide is located.
Start the scan
creator: Hendrik F HFvE Van Essen
